_: Canonical content for /pitch. Edit here, not in components. Per-audience overrides under `audiences.{key}.overrides`. Numbers verified against tagrides-pitch-deck.pdf + survey CSVs (210 responses) + Lagos route research (Nov-2024).

investor.label: Investor
investor.tagline: For investors evaluating TagRides as a Lagos-mobility opportunity.

use.label: Product & engineering
use.detail: Two engineers, app polish, driver dispatch + matching infra

use.label: Driver acquisition
use.detail: Onboarding incentives, training, vehicle inspections across Lekki-Epe + Mainland corridors

use.label: Marketing & growth
use.detail: Lagos awareness campaign, referral economy, influencer partnerships

use.label: Lagos regulatory + ops
use.detail: LASRRA, LASDRI, insurance, legal counsel, Owode-Ogombo HQ

judge.label: Competition Judge
judge.tagline: For pitch-competition juries — emphasis on impact, traction, and the team.

customer.label: Customer
customer.tagline: For Lagosians who want to know what TagRides actually does for them.

cover.title: Their route. Your ride.
cover.subtitle: Lagos route-share — drivers heading your way pick you up, you agree the fare directly, and you pay only for your leg of the trip.

problem.title: Lagos commutes punish everyone — and reward no one.
problem.subtitle: Riders overpay or suffer. Drivers waste empty seats. Nothing in between exists.

personas.name: Fatima
pain: Pays ₦4,500–₦7,400 daily for Bolt to feel safe — 6–10× what a danfo costs.
Loses 30+ hours a week to traffic and stop-and-go pickups.
Doesn't trust danfos: no records, no accountability, no comfort.

personas.name: Emeka
pain: Drives Lagos roads daily — 3 empty seats every trip.
Bolt detours kill his schedule and his fuel margin.
No way to earn from the route he's already taking.

lagosCost.eyebrow: WHAT IT COSTS TO MOVE IN LAGOS
lagosCost.title: Ride-hailing is 3–10× more expensive than the alternative.
lagosCost.subtitle: 30 routes researched, Nov 2024. Same start. Same destination. Same time of day. The gap is the market.

stats.label: Average time a Lagosian spends in traffic
Cars per kilometer in Lagos (global avg: 11)
Annual economic loss from Lagos congestion
Of the world can't afford ride-hailing as daily transport

lagosCost.source: TagRides field research — 30 Lagos routes, n=30 respondents, Nov 2024.

solution.title: Tag along the routes drivers already drive.
solution.subtitle: We don't pay drivers to detour. We don't reroute riders. We match riders to drivers already heading where they're going — and they share the leg of the trip they overlap on.

mechanics.title: Driver sets their route
mechanics.detail: Mainland → Island, 7:30am. The system understands it as a path through the city, not a guess.

mechanics.title: Riders request a leg of it
mechanics.detail: Anyone on or near that line can tag along — pickup, drop-off, fare-share, all in-app.

mechanics.title: Fare is agreed, not metered
mechanics.detail: Driver offers. Rider accepts or negotiates. No surge. No commission games. Just two people splitting a real cost.

product.title: Built in Flutter. Live on AWS. Already running.
product.subtitle: Four core flows shipped, 200+ Lagosians on the inside loop, signing up weekly.

market.title: A $2B informal market, ready for digitisation.
market.subtitle: African ride-sharing is forecast to grow $2.1B → $3.4B by 2029 (Statista). Nigeria alone has 36M daily road users with smartphones — a market value of $2B mostly untouched by ride-hailing pricing.

model.title: Earn from the trips that already happen.
model.subtitle: Two clean revenue lines, both compounding with usage. No surge games, no driver-poaching wars.

streams.label: Commission on rider fares
streams.detail: Charged on every confirmed tag-along ride. Driver and rider both see the cut up-front.

streams.label: Online services & APIs
streams.detail: Route data, driver verification, and fare-estimate APIs licensed to logistics + fintech partners.

pricing.label: Distance-based base
pricing.detail: Per-km tariff visible before the rider taps Request.

pricing.label: Time-of-day modifier
pricing.detail: Higher during rush — but capped at the driver's accepted offer, never algorithmic surge.

pricing.label: Rider–driver negotiation
pricing.detail: The fare is a conversation, not a verdict. Riders can counter; drivers can accept or decline.

competitive.title: Nothing in the market does what we do.
competitive.subtitle: Ride-hail democratised the taxi. Tag Rides democratises the commute itself.

differentiation.line: TagRides democratises ride-hailing. UberX monetises it.
TagRides scales through the crowd, not through state-owned fleets.
TagRides is shared mobility on demand — not shared transport on a schedule.

traction.title: Backed, built, and quietly accumulating users.
traction.subtitle: Live MVP. Real surveys. Capital and credits already secured.

proof.label: Live MVP on AWS
proof.context: Flutter app, Go services, MQTT real-time

team.title: Operators who've shipped.
team.subtitle: Mobile, backend, and Lagos network — built in.

members.name: Oniya Olaiya
members.bio: Ships the Flutter rider+driver app. ALX Ventures alum. Lagos-born; relocating from EC2-development to in-market lead.

members.name: Oniya Adesubomi
members.bio: Go services, geolocation pipeline, MQTT real-time, MongoDB infrastructure.

members.name: Efemena Abraham
members.bio: Lagos tech-ecosystem operator. Apex Network introductions across logistics + payments.

members.name: Pst. Wale Olasoji
members.bio: Corporate operations + Lagos community gateway. Fuel-economics + driver-network access.

financials.title: Profitable from year two on a 5-year run.
financials.subtitle: Conservative — driver supply is the rate-limit, not demand. Cohort retention modeled at 35% by month 3.

items.label: MVP live on AWS — Flutter + Go

impact.title: Formalising the informal — and making cities work.
impact.subtitle: Tag Rides turns everyday drivers into verified entrepreneurs, digitising a $2B informal transport market.

outcomes.label: New income opportunities unlocked across 5 years

ask.title: We're raising $350K to make Lagos the proof.
ask.subtitle: Pre-seed. Eighteen months of runway. Build the Lekki-Epe corridor into a self-sustaining tag-along economy — then take the template Africa-wide.
